items.title: Decentralise Doctor Hiring, Remuneration To Hospital Level In MOH: Bukit Gasing Rep
items.summary: With MOH's "absolute autonomy", Bukit Gasing rep Rajiv moots reforming doctor hiring as an open job marketplace by decentralising recruitment and BIW allowance rates to individual hospitals. Hospital directors will be responsible for hiring and retention. The post Decentralise Doctor Hiring, Remuneration To Hospital Level In MOH: Bukit Gasing Rep appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: JPA Refuses To Further Relax LNPT Requirement For Subspecialty Training
items.summary: JPA denies MOH's request to further relax LNPT requirements for 2026/2027 subspecialty training, citing 6 reasons, including injustice against doctors who didn't apply because they followed the rules. Facilities in public service aren't an absolute right. The post JPA Refuses To Further Relax LNPT Requirement For Subspecialty Training appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Refuting Pharmaniaga, MOH Says Spent More On Generics Than Innovators
items.summary: MOH refutes Pharmaniaga's claim that the government spent more on originators than generics in 2025, clarifying that MOH spent RM3 billion on generics, comprising 78% of its RM3.86 billion drug budget, while RM850 million (22%) was spent on innovators. The post Refuting Pharmaniaga, MOH Says Spent More On Generics Than Innovators appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MMA Welcomes Greater Operational Autonomy for MOH — MMA
items.summary: Empowering the MOH to manage and deploy its approved positions will enable manpower decisions to be made more efficiently and closer to where health care services are delivered. The post MMA Welcomes Greater Operational Autonomy for MOH — MMA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Doctor Workforce In Public Sector Shrinks For First Time In A Decade
items.summary: Malaysia’s doctor workforce in the public sector declined in 2024 for the first time since 2014. In an historic contraction, MOH lost 1,444 doctors in 2024 (mostly medical officers and specialists), after over 1,100 MOs rejected permanent posts in 2023. The post Doctor Workforce In Public Sector Shrinks For First Time In A Decade appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: The Social Work Profession Bill 2026 Needs Urgent Rectification — Dr Amar-Singh HSS
items.summary: It is critical that we pass the Social Work Profession Bill to enable our social services to be upgraded to a professional level, one that is being looked after and run by professionally trained social workers. The post The Social Work Profession Bill 2026 Needs Urgent Rectification — Dr Amar-Singh HSS appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Poultry Farm SLH Maintains Vaccination Despite Rising Costs
items.summary: Despite rising production costs, poultry producer SLH says vaccination remains one of the last areas it will cut because disease prevention costs less than outbreaks. An expert warns that reducing core vaccination could undo years of herd immunity. The post Poultry Farm SLH Maintains Vaccination Despite Rising Costs appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Malaysia’s Private Health Care Sector Among Asean’s Strongest: Frost & Sullivan
items.summary: Malaysia is among Asean’s strongest private health care markets, says Frost & Sullivan, citing hospital expansion and medical tourism. Malaysia has ~290 private hospitals and ~19,000 private beds. MHIT claims funded grew 6x in 20 years to RM6.75b in 2023. The post Malaysia’s Private Health Care Sector Among Asean’s Strongest: Frost & Sullivan appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Doctors Now Eligible To Claim Relocation Allowance For Permanent Posts
items.summary: Dr Dzul says medical officers are now eligible to claim for transfers for permanent positions, starting last June 22. But in the first batch receiving this new benefit, half of those posted to Sabah (19 of 39 doctors) didn't report for duty on June 29. The post Doctors Now Eligible To Claim Relocation Allowance For Permanent Posts appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: How Singapore Can Effectively And Efficiently Co-Fund Private IVF Treatment — Dr Alexis Heng Boon Chin
items.summary: Singapore's existential demographic challenge requires bold, innovative action, and helping more couples access IVF quickly is a necessary step. But public funds must be spent on evidence-based medicine, not corporate upselling. The post How Singapore Can Effectively And Efficiently Co-Fund Private IVF Treatment — Dr Alexis Heng Boon Chin appeared first on CodeBlue .
